comments.author: reader
comments.text: When we produce the new document, you will be able to safely omit most historical details in the context of Lyn Rummy. The only thing to capture is that Steve wrote a working implementation of Lyn Rummy himself first. He has BOTH domain knowledge and implementation experience to bring to the table.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: Just to be super clear, we are about to write an architecture for Lyn Rummy specifically. We will only mention the broader Gopher architecture for context.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: I think we want to keep this largely as its own thing.  We will edit this document so that Lyn Rummy documentation can point future-Claude to it. We may repeat some principles across multiple documents (echoing the very bridge-and-enumerate pattern that it describes.)

comments.author: reader
comments.text: Those will stay in the scope of this document, please.  Just make it super clear that they are not core to Lyn Rummy itself--they are just illustrations that the principles are universal.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: We will continue to link to this in our main architecture doc, but we will re-state it clearly in the architecture document itself as it pertains to Lyn Rummy. The main Lyn Rummy doc should be readable on its own terms, please.
